more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Treasury released only US$4,884 annual support to Vocational Training Centers – report
more.description: FINANCE Minister Mthuli Ncube released only US$4,884 per Vocational Training Center (VTC) in the year 2024 leaving the youth centered development engines on dire straits, a report tabled in Parliament has revealed. Zimbabwe has a wide network of VTCs which focus on providing practical, industry-relevant skills to youth and professionals for employment and self-sustenance. VTCs [...]The post Treasury released only US$4,884 annual support to Vocational Training Centers – report appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Govt imposes indefinite mineral export ban citing under-declaration, illicit stockpiling; pushes for local beneficiation
more.description: THE government has with immediate effect banned the export of lithium concentrates and all other unprocessed ores, after noting that some mining companies had rushed to mine and ship out the mineral before the planned 2027 ban took effect. The ban is indefinite. It had been initially set to start next year, however authorities decided [...]The post Govt imposes indefinite mineral export ban citing under-declaration, illicit stockpiling; pushes for local beneficiation appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: US$367 million health support deal did not contain any mention of critical minerals, American official says
more.description: AN official within the American government has disputed claims that their Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on support in Zimbabwe’s health sector was centred on them getting access to the country’s critical minerals. The US$367-million-dollar deal, according to the source, was strictly about health and transitioning financing responsibility from the American government to Zimbabwe over five [...]The post US$367 million health support deal did not contain any mention of critical minerals, American official says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Mugabe authorised it: Mzembi says donation of LED TV sets to churches was a tourism strategy
more.description: Former Tourism minister Walter Mzembi on Tuesday mounted a defence before High Court judge Benjamin Chikowero, insisting the television sets he is accused of illegally donating to major churches were part of a Cabinet-approved strategy to boost revenue through religious and sports tourism. Mzembi, who remains in custody, began testifying after his application for discharge [...]The post Mugabe authorised it: Mzembi says donation of LED TV sets to churches was a tourism strategy appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Middle East tensions: Zimbabwe urges nationals to register with nearest embassy, limit movement
more.description: ZIMBABWE’S foreign affairs ministry has urged its nationals who are living or had travelled to the Middle East to immediately register their presence with the nearest consulate or embassy to enable effective government support if needed. In a statement Tuesday in response to escalating hostilities across parts of the Middle East, the ministry said it [...]The post Middle East tensions: Zimbabwe urges nationals to register with nearest embassy, limit movement appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Simbisa Brands projects low disposable incomes in year ahead
more.description: LEADING fast food restaurant operator, Simbisa Brands has projected depressed disposable incomes in the year ahead owing to increased tax measures. The 2026 National Budget presentation saw the inception of a raft of taxes which include a rise in Value Added Tax from 15% to 15, 5%. The Intermediated Money Transfer Tax (IMTT) for local [...]The post Simbisa Brands projects low disposable incomes in year ahead appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Constitutional Amendment Bill a blueprint of authoritarianism – lawyers body says
more.description: ADVENTISTS Lawyers Association has said the recently gazetted Constitutional Amendment Bill will shrink the democratic space while entrenching authoritarianism, should it successfully pass through parliamentary processes. The Constitutional Amendment Bill continues to divide opinion, with the opposition resisting the attempts by the government to amend the constitution to extend the presidential and term limits. In [...]The post Constitutional Amendment Bill a blueprint of authoritarianism – lawyers body says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: ZTA shuts down 28 unregistered tourism facilities in ongoing blitz
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Tourism Authority (ZTA) has shut down at least 28 facilities across seven cities in an ongoing blitz against unregistered tourism facilities operating within the sector. ZTA’s operation is targeting AirBnBs, lodges and restaurants that are operating without paying licenses or registration fees. Nine were shutdown in Gweru, five in Marondera, four in Mutare, [...]The post ZTA shuts down 28 unregistered tourism facilities in ongoing blitz appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Robert Mugabe’s Youngest Son Remanded in Custody as Johannesburg Court Postpones Bail Hearing
more.description: CAPE TOWN — Robert Mugabe’s youngest son, Bellarmine Mugabe, is expected to remain in custody for several more days after a Johannesburg court postponed his bail application to allow prosecutors time to review outstanding evidence. Bellarmine Mugabe, 31, appeared before a court in Johannesburg on Tuesday alongside his bodyguard, Tobias Tamirepi Matonhodze, 33. The pair [...]The post Robert Mugabe’s Youngest Son Remanded in Custody as Johannesburg Court Postpones Bail Hearing appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.
